title: Custom attributes should be marked with System.AttributeUsageAttribute
description: Custom attributes should be marked with "System.AttributeUsageAttribute"Analyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode Smellapi-designWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWhen defining custom attributes,AttributeUsageAttributemust be used to indicate where the attribute can be applied. This will:indicate how the attribute can be usedprevent it from being used at invalid locationsAvailable In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Classes that inherit from System.Attribute but do not have [AttributeUsage] attribute on the line immediately before the class declaration
logic.triggering_conditions: Class inherits from 'Attribute' AND does not have '[AttributeUsage]' attribute AND is not abstract AND is not in System namespace
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude abstract classes (base classes for inheritance), classes in System.* namespaces (built-in attributes), classes that inherit from already-attributed base classes
logic.reasoning: Custom attributes must have [AttributeUsage] to indicate where they can be applied and prevent misuse. The scanner extracts both ClassDeclaration and Attribute nodes, and the custom function correlates them by line numbers to detect missing AttributeUsage. Abstract base attributes are excluded as they're meant for inheritance. System namespace attributes are built-in and don't require user-defined AttributeUsage.

should_trigger: public class ValidatorAttribute : Attribute { public Type ValidatorType { get; set; } }
should_not_trigger: namespace System { public class InternalAttribute : Attribute { } }
